items.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in airstrike, Trump says
items.standfirst: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.
items.content: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

items.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
items.standfirst: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.
items.content: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)

items.title: Trump says alleged leader of Tren de Aragua gang killed in U.S. strike
items.standfirst: The president said the military strike that killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, who’s known as Niño Guerrero, was carried out in coordination with Venezuela.
items.content: The president said the military strike that killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, who’s known as Niño Guerrero, was carried out in coordination with Venezuela.

items.title: Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike
items.standfirst: President says Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores killed in ‘swift and lethal’ military strike with help from VenezuelaThe US military has killed a leader in the Venezuelan street gang Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, with the help of Venezuela, Donald Trump announced on Friday.“At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, the infamous leader of Tren De Aragua, one of the most bloodthirsty Terrorist Organizations on Planet Earth,” Trump wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...
items.content: President says Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores killed in ‘swift and lethal’ military strike with help from VenezuelaThe US military has killed a leader in the Venezuelan street gang Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, with the help of Venezuela, Donald Trump announced on Friday.“At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, the infamous leader of Tren De Aragua, one of the most bloodthirsty Terrorist Organizations on Planet Earth,” Trump wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...

items.content: President Donald Trump has said a "swift and lethal kinetic" US military strike has killed "the infamous leader" of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua street gang.

items.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
items.standfirst: The US president's comments come as Iran says an exact date has not been decided, but it "will not be tomorrow".
items.content: The US president's comments come as Iran says an exact date has not been decided, but it "will not be tomorrow".

items.content: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened "immediately," once the memorandum of understanding is signed.

items.content: Both sides have indicated a deal to end the war is close, but have diverged on details and timeline for a breakthrough.

items.content: Protesters gathered at Belfast City Hall for a demonstration against racism, after the past week of violence and disorder.

items.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting that followed stabbing

items.content: Thousands of people on Saturday gathered for an anti-racism rally in the Northern Irish city of Belfast after unrest sparked by a horrific stabbing. France 24's Hervé Amoric tells us more.

clusters.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Name Removed From Kennedy Center

items.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
items.standfirst: Crews erected scaffolding on Friday as onlookers gathered into the evening, though storms delayed the work until Saturday.
items.content: Crews erected scaffolding on Friday as onlookers gathered into the evening, though storms delayed the work until Saturday.

items.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, court filing says
items.standfirst: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.
items.content: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

items.title: Workers remove Trump’s name from Kennedy Center after court ruling
items.standfirst: Judge says Trump-backed board's move to add president's name unlawful, requires Congressional approval.
items.content: Judge says Trump-backed board's move to add president's name unlawful, requires Congressional approval.

items.title: Kennedy Center removes Trump's name from the building
items.standfirst: Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
items.content: Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)

ai_summary: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center

Removal completed early Saturday

clusters.title: Warner Bros $111bn sale to Paramount approved by US justice department

items.title: Warner Bros $111bn sale to Paramount approved by US justice department
items.standfirst: The approval marks a key development in the merger that will reshape media, allowing the continue of the takeover of the Hollywood studio, which owns CNN and HBO.
items.content: The approval marks a key development in the merger that will reshape media, allowing the continue of the takeover of the Hollywood studio, which owns CNN and HBO.

items.title: US justice department approves $111bn merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery
items.standfirst: Deal still under UK scrutiny with new investigation, and could face lawsuit from state attorneys generalDonald Trump’s Department of Justice has decided to approve the $111bn merger of Paramount Skydance, controlled by the Ellison family, and Warner Bros Discovery, the parent company of networks like CNN and HBO.The deal was approved by the justice department’s anti-trust division after months of review, and despite the concerns of many people in the entertainment and media industries who believe it will hurt competition by reducing the number of film studios and – most likely – merging two news networks, Paramount’s CBS News and CNN. Continue reading...
items.content: Deal still under UK scrutiny with new investigation, and could face lawsuit from state attorneys generalDonald Trump’s Department of Justice has decided to approve the $111bn merger of Paramount Skydance, controlled by the Ellison family, and Warner Bros Discovery, the parent company of networks like CNN and HBO.The deal was approved by the justice department’s anti-trust division after months of review, and despite the concerns of many people in the entertainment and media industries who believe it will hurt competition by reducing the number of film studios and – most likely – merging two news networks, Paramount’s CBS News and CNN. Continue reading...

items.title: Paramount-Warner Brothers merger gets Justice Department approval
items.standfirst: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
items.content: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.

items.title: Justice Dept. approves Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
items.standfirst: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.
items.content: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN. (Image credit: Nick Ut)

items.title: Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros. Is Cleared by US DOJ
items.standfirst: The US Justice Department has closed an antitrust probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., saying the deal “is not likely” to hurt consumers or competition in the film and television industry.
items.content: The US Justice Department has closed an antitrust probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., saying the deal “is not likely” to hurt consumers or competition in the film and television industry.

ai_summary: US justice department approves Warner Bros sale
$111bn merger with Paramount approved
Deal still under UK scrutiny
Merger to reshape media industry
Concerns raised about impact on competition

clusters.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns

items.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
items.standfirst: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.
items.content: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.

items.title: Anthropic disables access to top-tier AI models after US ban on foreign use
items.standfirst: AI giant Anthropic said in a statement released Friday that it had disabled its most advanced AI models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, citing unspecified national security concerns.
items.content: AI giant Anthropic said in a statement released Friday that it had disabled its most advanced AI models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, citing unspecified national security concerns.

clusters.title: Anger over planned luxury resorts in Albania, including one linked to Trump's son-in-law

items.title: Anger over planned luxury resorts in Albania, including one linked to Trump's son-in-law
items.standfirst: Around 200 protesters have torn down fencing surrounding a controversial luxury development in Albania, as demonstrations continue over another planned resort linked ​to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.
items.content: Around 200 protesters have torn down fencing surrounding a controversial luxury development in Albania, as demonstrations continue over another planned resort linked ​to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

items.title: Albanians protest against another luxury development on Adriatic coast
items.standfirst: Fencing removed at environmentally sensitive site, mirroring protests against Trump son-in-law’s projectAbout 200 protesters on Saturday tore down metal and razor-wire fences surrounding a luxury development site on Albania’s Adriatic coast, in another sign of growing anger against construction in environmentally sensitive areas.Albanians have been protesting for weeks against a planned luxury resort backed by a company linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of Donald Trump, near Vlora, which is famed for its flamingos and a turtle nesting site. Continue reading...
items.content: Fencing removed at environmentally sensitive site, mirroring protests against Trump son-in-law’s projectAbout 200 protesters on Saturday tore down metal and razor-wire fences surrounding a luxury development site on Albania’s Adriatic coast, in another sign of growing anger against construction in environmentally sensitive areas.Albanians have been protesting for weeks against a planned luxury resort backed by a company linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of Donald Trump, near Vlora, which is famed for its flamingos and a turtle nesting site. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Protesters tear down fencing at resort site
Demonstrations against luxury developments
Concerns about environmental impact
Project linked to Trump's son-in-law
Protests continue in Albania

clusters.title: Mourners line Bangkok streets to pay respects to Thailand’s Princess Bha

items.title: Mourners line Bangkok streets to pay respects to Thailand’s Princess Bha
items.standfirst: Funeral procession travels to palace as people remember royal’s campaigning and work for underprivilegedAs the sun began to set on the golden spires and gilded finials of Bangkok’s Grand Palace, the gates were open, waiting for the return of a princess.Since December 2022, Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been in hospital, having collapsed while out training her dogs. After nearly four years in a coma, the princess died earlier this week. Continue reading...
items.content: Funeral procession travels to palace as people remember royal’s campaigning and work for underprivilegedAs the sun began to set on the golden spires and gilded finials of Bangkok’s Grand Palace, the gates were open, waiting for the return of a princess.Since December 2022, Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been in hospital, having collapsed while out training her dogs. After nearly four years in a coma, the princess died earlier this week. Continue reading...

items.title: Thai princess dies aged 47
items.standfirst: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha has died after nearly four years in a coma, aged 47.
items.content: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha has died after nearly four years in a coma, aged 47.

items.title: Thailand’s Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies aged 47 after years in a coma
items.standfirst: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol’s health had worsened since she was hospitalised in December 2022 with heart problems that left her gravely illThe eldest child of Thailand’s King Maha Vajiralongkorn has died aged 47, the palace has said, after nearly four years in a coma.Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, known in Thailand as Princess Bha, had been in hospital since December 2022 when she became gravely ill after having heart problems while out training her dogs. Continue reading...
items.content: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol’s health had worsened since she was hospitalised in December 2022 with heart problems that left her gravely illThe eldest child of Thailand’s King Maha Vajiralongkorn has died aged 47, the palace has said, after nearly four years in a coma.Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, known in Thailand as Princess Bha, had been in hospital since December 2022 when she became gravely ill after having heart problems while out training her dogs. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies aged 47
Princess had been in coma for nearly four years
Funeral procession travels to palace
Mourners pay respects to the princess
Princess was known for her charity work
